auth.notes: Append ?api_key=KEY or &api_key=KEY to every request. No header-based auth supported. DEMO_KEY available for low-volume testing.

rate_limits.enforcement: Temporary 1-hour block on key/IP when exceeded

endpoints.name: search_foods_get

optional_params.name: dataType
optional_params.type: array via repeated param or CSV

optional_params.name: brandOwner
optional_params.note: Branded foods only; requires exact stored string match

endpoints.response_shape: {totalHits: int, currentPage: int, totalPages: int, pageList: int[], foodSearchCriteria: {...}, aggregations: {...}, foods: [SearchResultFood]}
endpoints.nutrient_basis: per_100g for Foundation/SR Legacy; per_100g stored (LCCS-derived) for Branded
endpoints.nutrient_format: flat: {nutrientId, nutrientName, nutrientNumber: string, unitName, value, derivationCode, derivationDescription, rank, indentLevel, foodNutrientId}
quirks: Empty query returns HTTP 200 with all 468K foods — not treated as error; validate non-empty before calling
numberOfResultsPerPage in criteria echo is always 50 regardless of pageSize sent
dataType accepts both repeated params (?dataType=A&dataType=B) and CSV (?dataType=A,B) identically
Multi-dataType results skew toward Branded on page 1 due to relevance scoring
brandOwner requires exact string match as stored in database
foodNutrients uses 'value' (not 'amount') and flat shape — different from /food/{id} full format
score field (Elasticsearch relevance) is present but undocumented
aggregations field present but undocumented

endpoints.name: search_foods_post

optional_body_fields.name: tradeChannel
optional_body_fields.note: POST only — not available on GET

quirks: dataType body field must be array of strings, not comma-separated string
Adds tradeChannel, startDate, endDate params not available on GET version
brandOwner exact-match constraint same as GET version

endpoints.name: get_food_by_id

response_shape.full_non_branded: {fdcId, description, dataType, publicationDate, foodClass, ndbNumber, isHistoricalReference, scientificName, foodCategory, foodNutrients:[nested], foodPortions, foodComponents, foodAttributes, nutrientConversionFactors, inputFoods}
response_shape.full_branded: {fdcId, description, dataType, publicationDate, brandOwner, brandName, gtinUpc, ingredients, servingSize, servingSizeUnit, householdServingFullText, packageWeight, brandedFoodCategory, marketCountry, modifiedDate, availableDate, dataSource, discontinuedDate, labelNutrients, foodNutrients:[nested], foodPortions, foodComponents, foodAttributes, foodUpdateLog}

endpoints.nutrient_basis: per_100g (Foundation, SR Legacy, Survey); Branded foodNutrients derived from serving-size via LCCS derivation code; labelNutrients (Branded only) are per serving

nutrient_format.full: nested: {type, id, amount, nutrient:{id, number:string, name, rank, unitName}, foodNutrientDerivation:{id, code, description, foodNutrientSource}, dataPoints, min, max, median, nutrientAnalysisDetails}
nutrient_format.abridged: flat: {number:string, name, amount, unitName, derivationCode, derivationDescription}

quirks: 404 returns empty body (no JSON) — do not attempt json.parse on 404 response
Full foodNutrients array includes category header objects with no 'amount' field — filter by presence of 'amount'
nutrient.number is a string ('208'), not integer
publicationDate format inconsistent: M/D/YYYY (older) vs YYYY-MM-DD (newer Foundation)
labelNutrients present ONLY on Branded dataType — absent on Foundation, SR Legacy, Survey
fdcId 1750340 is Foundation (Apples), not Branded despite docs example labeling it otherwise
Abridged foodNutrients uses flat format (different field names: 'number'/'amount' vs full 'nutrient.number'/'amount')

endpoints.name: get_foods_by_ids
endpoints.response_shape: Bare array: [FoodItem, FoodItem, ...]  — NO envelope object
quirks: Returns bare array, not {foods:[...]}
Max 20 fdcIds per call

endpoints.name: post_foods_by_ids
endpoints.response_shape: Bare array: [FoodItem, FoodItem, ...]  — NO envelope object
quirks: Returns bare array, not {foods:[...]}
Empty fdcIds:[] returns {} (empty object) with HTTP 200 — not an error response
Max 20 fdcIds per spec

endpoints.name: list_foods_get
endpoints.response_shape: Bare array: [AbridgedFoodItem, ...]  — NO pagination envelope; no totalHits/totalPages
endpoints.nutrient_format: Always flat: {number:string, name, amount, unitName, derivationCode, derivationDescription}
quirks: Returns bare array with no pagination metadata — cannot determine total count or pages
Always returns abridged-format items regardless of any format param
Use /foods/search for paginated results with totalHits

endpoints.name: list_foods_post
quirks: Same quirks as GET /foods/list

endpoints.name: get_openapi_spec
quirks: Authoritative schema source; use for code generation and field validation

datatypes.Foundation: USDA ARS analytically measured; highest data density; per 100g; includes sub-sample acquisition details; bi-annual updates
datatypes.SR Legacy: USDA Standard Reference final release (April 2018); frozen dataset; per 100g; use for historical compatibility
datatypes.Branded: Commercial products from manufacturer labels; largest dataset; includes labelNutrients (per serving), servingSize, ingredients, GTIN/UPC; bi-annual updates
datatypes.Survey (FNDDS): NHANES dietary survey aligned; updated every 2 years; includes food codes; per 100g
datatypes.Experimental: Listed in browse UI; no download releases; minimal data

full_nested.note: Category headers included (no amount field) — filter by presence of 'amount'

flat_search.note: Uses 'value' not 'amount' — critical difference from other formats

labeled_nutrients_shape.availability: Branded foods only — absent on Foundation, SR Legacy, Survey

error.message: An invalid api_key was supplied. Get one at https://api.nal.usda.gov:443

empty_fdcids.note: Empty object — not an error response
